title: Onboarding crashes in prod release: cwd-relative priv path in starter_data
description: starter_data.ex:93 does File.read!(priv/recipe_catalog.json) with a cwd-relative path. Works in dev; in a release cwd differs and priv lives under the app lib dir -> File.Error, so new-account onboarding fails in production. Use Application.app_dir(:grocery_planner, ...).
acceptance_criteria:
- Path resolved via Application.app_dir
- Test asserts catalog loads via the app-dir path
- Grep confirms no other cwd-relative priv reads in lib/
close_reason: Fixed: catalog path resolved via Application.app_dir(:grocery_planner, ...); regression test changes cwd to tmp and loads the catalog (verified red against old code); grep confirms no other cwd-relative priv reads in lib/ (receipts_live and process_receipt already use :code.priv_dir).

title: Recipe import creates duplicate grocery items (nil-actor silent empty read) and swallows ingredient failures
description: recipe_importer.ex:60 calls Inventory.list_grocery_items(tenant: account_id) with no actor and no authorize?: false - the documented actor-nil pitfall: relates_to_actor_via policy silently returns [], so find_or_create never finds and every import duplicates grocery items. Compare correct callers item_matcher.ex:110 and receipt_live.ex:1093. Additionally import_ingredients (recipe_importer.ex:32-54) runs a no-else with inside Enum.each, discards create_recipe_ingredient results, and returns :ok unconditionally - recipes import as success with missing ingredients.
acceptance_criteria:
- list_grocery_items call passes authorize?: false (or real actor)
- Re-importing the same recipe twice does not duplicate grocery items (test)
- Ingredient creation failures surface as an error result from import_recipe (test)
close_reason: Fixed both defects: list_grocery_items now passes authorize?: false (dedup works); import_ingredients returns {:error, _} on first failure via reduce_while, propagated through import_recipe. Both regression tests verified red against old code. 972 tests passing.

title: Email digest is non-functional 3 ways: unsupervised GenServer, TenantRequired read, bad Ash.load - fix or delete
description: Notifications.EmailScheduler (notifications/email_scheduler.ex) never runs: (1) GenServer absent from application.ex supervision tree; (2) send_daily_emails/0 reads multi-tenant NotificationPreference with no tenant and no allow_global -> raises TenantRequired (lines 43-45); (3) Ash.load! called on never-loaded associations preference.user/preference.account -> raises, also N+1 (lines 48-49; intent was Ash.load!(preference, [:user, :account])). Zero test coverage is why three stacked defects went unnoticed. Decide: fix and supervise it, or delete the module (303 lines).
acceptance_criteria:
- Decision recorded: keep or delete
- If keep: supervised in application.ex, tenant-correct read (allow_global read action or per-tenant iteration), associations loaded correctly, test proving a digest email is generated for a user with due notifications
- If delete: module and dead config removed
close_reason: Decision: DELETE. Module was referenced nowhere (never supervised, never configured, zero tests) and had never run; fixing it properly is a feature build with the wrong architecture anyway (hand-rolled scheduling vs existing Oban Cron, hardcoded 7AM UTC vs account timezones). Deleted 303 lines; nothing orphaned (ExpirationAlerts/RecipeSuggestions/Mailer used elsewhere). Rebuild requirements + the dead email_notifications_enabled toggle captured in follow-up grocery_planner-wl5.

title: Fix member role escalation: role-gate AccountMembership/User policies + settings_live guard
description: Any :member of a shared account can add a colluding :owner and remove the real owner (settings_live.ex:200-253 never checks actor role; role comes from params). Root cause: AccountMembership has blanket 'policy always()' (accounts/account_membership.ex:37-41) and User is unscoped (accounts/user.ex:104-108 - any actor can update any user email). ActorMemberOfAccount check already exists and is used on Recipe/Family resources. Not reachable via JSON API (Accounts domain not mounted) but directly clickable in the UI.
acceptance_criteria:
- AccountMembership create/update/destroy require actor to be owner/admin of the target account (policy-level)
- User read/update/destroy scoped to self
- settings_live send_invitation/remove_member pass actor and enforce role
- Test: a :member attempting to add an owner or remove the owner gets Forbidden
- Existing owner/admin flows still pass
close_reason: Fixed: AccountMembership create/update/destroy now require owner/admin role; User read/update/destroy scoped to self; settings_live enforces role + passes actor. Also fixed a related latent bug in Account's own update/destroy policy (broken relates_to_actor_via filter: option). 967 tests passing.

title: AI-006 Arc 2: staged AshOban pipeline with durable milestone state model
description:
Arc 2 of AI-006. Depends on Arc 1 (don't restage around a payload we know lies). Fixes cxk, c29, eeg.

Split the single after_action into stages (extract -> persist -> match -> categorise), each an AshOban trigger with scheduler_cron: false, enqueued via AshOban.run_trigger/3. Keeps use_tenant_from_record? tenancy handling at zero cron latency (faster than today: :process currently scans every minute before anything starts).

receipt.status becomes the furthest DURABLE MILESTONE (:pending -> :extracted -> :items_created -> :ready_for_review -> :confirmed), never in-flight activity - Oban already knows what's executing. New receipt.raw_extraction jsonb column is the inter-stage handoff (Oban args carry receipt_id only).

Each stage: check pre/postcondition (postcondition met -> no-op), do work, write results + advance milestone in ONE transaction (Ash.bulk_create transaction: :all), enqueue next. Plus one reconciler trigger (real cron, stream_with: :full_read because the where depends on time) for stranded records, and free Oban.Plugins.Lifeline (safe only once stages are idempotent).

OPEN before implementing: (a) :failed/:awaiting_ai are conditions not milestones - likely a separate condition field, else one enum means two things which is exactly what :processing does wrong; (b) categorise is degradable so :ready_for_review must not depend on it; (c) re-extraction semantics for the (receipt_id, line_no) unique index.
design: DECISION 2026-07-16: pipeline stages are hand-written Oban.Worker modules, NOT AshOban triggers. Reason: AshOban 0.7's generated worker (deps/ash_oban define_schedulers.ex) runs the action via Ash.bulk_update! and only returns :ok / raises / {:cancel, state-when-where-no-longer-matches}. It has NO snooze path and no action-driven cancel, so the §4b taxonomy (:unavailable->{:snooze,n}+:awaiting_ai; {:bad_input,_}->{:cancel,reason}) is impossible via triggers. Custom backoff/1 IS supported by triggers but that alone is insufficient. Custom workers give full snooze/cancel/backoff control; tenancy set explicitly from receipt.account_id + authorize?:false, covered by tenant-scoping tests. Reconciler stays an AshOban trigger (scheduled scan = AshOban's sweet spot; no snooze needed). User confirmed approach.
acceptance_criteria: No premature status; no duplicate items on retry; no receipts stranded in a nameless processing state; sidecar-down snoozes with :awaiting_ai visible in the UI rather than burning attempts; custom backoff/1 discounts snoozes
notes: NAMING SETTLED 2026-07-16: the milestone field is 'stage' (the existing 'status' column renamed), the health field is 'condition'. status is retired entirely. So the migration in step 1 is: rename status -> stage; values :pending->:pending, :processing->:pending, :completed->:ready_for_review, :failed-> stage: :pending + condition: :failed. Add condition (atom, default :ok) and failure_reason. PROD DATA IS DISPOSABLE (food.davewil.dev receipts are throwaway) - the lossy :failed mapping is fine, no data-driven backfill. Everywhere the earlier design notes say receipt.status / 'status enum' / status in [...], read 'stage'.
close_reason: Shipped: staged extract->persist->match->categorise as custom Oban workers (AshOban can't do action-driven snooze/cancel); stage+condition durable state model; raw_extraction jsonb handoff; line_no + partial unique index; §4b taxonomy (snooze+awaiting_ai / cancel+failed / error) with capped backoff; reconciler AshOban trigger + Lifeline; matching moved out of LiveView. Tests drive real workers incl. drain-based enqueue-chain assertion. 955 tests + integration green (d5c69d3).

title: AI-006 Arc 1: honest seam — classify AiClient errors, enrich the flat contract
description:
Arc 1 of AI-006 (specs/AI-006-receipt-pipeline-rework.md). Ships alone; unblocks 83o.

Two halves, both at the Elixir<->Python seam.

CLASSIFY (blocks the pipeline's failure taxonomy): AiClient.handle_response/1 collapses everything into {:error, term} - HTTP non-200 returns {:error, body} (a decoded map), transport failures return {:error, reason} (an exception struct). Callers cannot distinguish sidecar-down from bad-image, so snooze/cancel/retry cannot be chosen. process_receipt.ex:30-33 treats all errors identically. Return {:ok, payload} | {:error, :unavailable} (transport/502/503) | {:error, {:bad_input, d}} (4xx) | {:error, {:transient, d}} (other 5xx).

ENRICH (unblocks 83o): the flat ExtractionResponsePayload drops raw_ocr_text, overall_confidence and currency, and does not return model_version/processing_time_ms - all four have columns on Receipt and are always nil in prod. main.py:531-565 already HAS the full ExtractionResult in hand and discards these to fit the narrow shape. Add them. Then parse the flat shape only in receipt_processor (delete the nested clauses + the extraction["extraction"] envelope), and rewrite fixtures to the real wire shape - they currently assert against the rich endpoint the app does not call.
design:
Producer first, then consumer, then tests. Spec §4a + §5.

1. python_service/schemas.py — ExtractionResponsePayload gains: currency (str, ISO-4217), raw_ocr_text (str), overall_confidence (float). Surface model_version + processing_time_ms (already computed for the artifact write, just not returned).
2. python_service/main.py — populate them in ALL THREE branches (vLLM, Tesseract, mock). The Tesseract branch at :531-565 already holds the full ExtractionResult and discards them to fit the narrow shape (result.raw_ocr_text, result.overall_confidence, result.total.currency via MoneyInfo). Stop discarding. Keep ExtractedItem.price but add per-item currency or hoist currency to the payload — decide once, not per-field.
3. ai_client.ex — classify. handle_response/1 currently returns {:error, body} for HTTP non-200 (a decoded map) and {:error, reason} for transport failures (an exception struct); callers cannot tell them apart. Return: {:ok, payload} | {:error, :unavailable} (transport, 502, 503) | {:error, {:bad_input, detail}} (4xx) | {:error, {:transient, detail}} (other 5xx). This is what unblocks Arc 2's §4b taxonomy.
4. receipt_processor.ex — parse the FLAT shape only. Delete: the nested heads of parse_merchant/parse_total_amount/parse_purchase_date, the extraction["extraction"] envelope (:100), the dual reads in create_receipt_item_from_extraction (item["raw_text"] || item["name"], parse_money(item["unit_price"]) || parse_flat_money(item["price"])), and parse_money/1 once orphaned. Take currency from the payload; delete the hardcoded :USD in parse_flat_money (83o).
5. Tests — rewrite receipt_processor_test.exs fixtures to the real wire shape (deleting the nested ones is the deliverable, not cleanup). Add a CONTRACT TEST asserting the Elixir parser's expectations against the actual ExtractionResponsePayload fields: the whole defect class is two sides disagreeing while both look correct, and nothing currently fails when they diverge. Assert the §4a classification table at the AiClient boundary with Req.Test stubs returning real sidecar payloads.

Do NOT resurrect the deleted Contracts module (afb24d7 removed it correctly — atomize/1 ran String.to_existing_atom over server-supplied keys). Write normalisers that coerce, default, and never raise, in the style of the categorizer's parse_meal normalisers.
acceptance_criteria: AiClient returns classified errors; currency crosses the wire and parse_flat_money's hardcoded :USD is gone (83o); raw_ocr_text/extraction_confidence/model_version/processing_time_ms are populated on the wired path; fixtures use the flat shape
close_reason: Arc 1 shipped: AiClient classifies errors (§4a), flat contract enriched (currency/raw_ocr_text/overall_confidence/model_version/processing_time_ms), receipt_processor parses flat shape only, hardcoded USD gone (unblocks 83o), two-sided contract test. CI green (dc2aff9).

title: [epic] AI-006: receipt pipeline rework — staged AshOban workers, honest contract
description:
Rework the receipt OCR pipeline into staged AshOban triggers with a durable milestone state model. Design: specs/AI-006-receipt-pipeline-rework.md (sections 1-3 settled, 4-7 open).

Root cause of cxk: ReceiptProcessor.save_extraction_results/2 does the whole pipeline in one after_action with no durable state machine - sets status: :completed BEFORE creating items, creates them with an unkeyed Enum.each, and Task.starts categorisation unsupervised.

Underneath sits a proven contract mismatch: the app calls the flat /api/v1/extract-receipt scaffold endpoint, while receipt_processor parses the rich /api/v1/receipts/extract shape (orphaned - it needs image_path and ai-service does not mount receipt-uploads). Result: raw_ocr_text, extraction_confidence, model_version, processing_time_ms are always nil on the wired path despite having columns, and parse_flat_money hardcodes :USD because the flat contract carries no currency (= 83o's root cause). Fixtures test the endpoint the app does not call.

Supersedes the pipeline built in AI-003 (4a51ab8). Absorbs cxk, c29, eeg; unblocks 83o. No Oban Pro, no broker, Reactor deferred (see org-brain card reactor-is-not-durable-...).
design: Staged AshOban triggers with scheduler_cron: false, enqueued via AshOban.run_trigger/3 (keeps use_tenant_from_record? tenancy handling, zero cron latency). receipt.status = furthest durable milestone; each stage checks pre/postcondition, writes results + advances milestone in ONE transaction (Ash.bulk_create transaction: :all), enqueues next. Idempotent by construction. Plus one reconciler trigger (real cron, stream_with: :full_read) for stranded records. New receipt.raw_extraction jsonb column is the inter-stage handoff and replaces the sidecar's write-only ai_artifacts store.
acceptance_criteria: cxk's three symptoms gone (no premature status, no dupes on retry, no stranded receipts); currency crosses the wire (83o unblocked); fixtures use the real flat wire shape; boundary test drives upload -> :ready_for_review

title: Task: reclaim disk on the docker host before first deploy (83% full)
description:

Reclaim space first: docker system prune / dangling images from prior slackex-pattern deploys are the likely bulk; check journal logs and old image tags too. HITL unless agent SSH to 192.168.1.102 is approved for this ticket. Answer records space reclaimed and free headroom.
close_reason: Owner pruned the docker host. Result: / at 65% (was 83%), 11G free of 32G — comfortable for the ~2-3GB first deploy (app+sidecar+pgvector images + pg/HF/uploads volumes). Note: docker reports 11.28GB 'reclaimable' images but 27/28 are ACTIVE (other running apps), so that is not real headroom; 11G free is. Unblocks tdu.6.

title: Prototype: docker-compose.prod.yml + caddy labels for the three-service stack
description:
## Question

What does the prod compose actually look like? Draft a concrete docker-compose.prod.yml to react to: app (default + proxy networks, caddy labels for food.davewil.dev), postgres (implicit default network only, image per the Postgres research ticket), CPU-only sidecar (vLLM off, Tesseract on, MiniLM embeddings; CPU/memory limits sized to the VM; HF model cache decision — bake vs volume), volumes (pg data, receipt uploads), healthchecks (DB-aware /ready per org-brain), env/secrets surface for the host .env. slackex at /root/slackex/ is the reference.
close_reason: Prototype on branch prototype/prod-compose; cache=volume, sidecar 2cpu/2g, AI categorization off pending DeepInfra review. See resolution comment.

comments.author: davewil
comments.text:
RESOLUTION:

Prototype delivered and reviewed with owner: docker-compose.prod.yml + .env.prod.example on branch prototype/prod-compose (commit 'prototype: prod compose + caddy labels for three-service stack (tdu.4)'). Validates with docker compose config. Promoted to main by tdu.6.

Decisions settled in review:
- HF model cache: named volume at /home/appuser/.cache (MiniLM ~90MB downloads once at first boot, survives recreates). Bake-into-image rejected for now; revisit only if first boot is painful.
- Sidecar ceiling: cpus 2 / memory 2g — host LXC has 12 CPUs allocated (verified in Proxmox), so this is conservative.
- Day-one flags: RECEIPT_PROCESSING_ENABLED=true, SEMANTIC_SEARCH_ENABLED=true, AI_CATEGORIZATION_ENABLED=false pending a DeepInfra review (slackex pattern) — filed as separate backlog issue, out of map scope.
- TIDEWAVE_ENABLED=false pinned (config.py defaults it TRUE — dev tool must not ship on).
- App→sidecar dependency: service_started + required:false — sidecar down = degraded, never blocks boot.

Facts later tickets depend on:
- App runner image has NO curl — the compose healthcheck needs curl added to the runner apt-get line (noted on grocery_planner-c29).
- Two one-time volume chowns at provision (uploads→65534/nobody, hf-cache→1000/appuser) + migrate command documented in the compose header comments — feed the ops runbook fog item.
- Docker host disk at 83% — new task ticket gates first deploy (blocks tdu.6).

title: Decide: force_ssl/HSTS yes — but does Postgres TLS survive scoping?
description:
## Question

Issue grocery_planner-4p7 bundles force_ssl/HSTS with Postgres TLS. On the homelab, app and postgres share a single-host docker network — does Postgres TLS buy anything there, or does it get dropped from 4p7's scope? Resolve and amend 4p7 accordingly. Recommendation going in: force_ssl/HSTS yes (public exposure), PG TLS no (loopback-equivalent docker network, no untrusted hop).
close_reason: Decided: force_ssl [rewrite_on: :x_forwarded_proto, hsts: true]; Postgres TLS dropped from 4p7 scope (single-host docker network, no untrusted hop).

comments.author: davewil
comments.text:
RESOLUTION:

**force_ssl/HSTS: yes. Postgres TLS: dropped from scope.**

- Postgres TLS dropped (owner confirmed): app ↔ postgres ride the compose default network on a single host — no untrusted hop; an attacker who can sniff the docker bridge already owns the host where TLS keys would live. Cost is real (postgres image ships no certs → mint/mount/rotate) for ~zero threat coverage. Revisit only if the DB ever moves off-host.
- force_ssl shape (follows from tdu.2's chain — app never terminates TLS, caddy does): force_ssl: [rewrite_on: [:x_forwarded_proto], hsts: true] on the prod endpoint. rewrite_on is mandatory or the site redirect-loops behind the proxy.
- Deliberate omissions: no subdomains: true (other davewil.dev hosts manage themselves), no preload (irreversible, pointless for a household app), no CF Always-Use-HTTPS dependency (optional extra line for the smoke checklist's CF API checks, not part of this decision).
- grocery_planner-4p7 amended accordingly: force_ssl/HSTS only.

title: Decide: Cloudflare proxy mode and the TLS chain to caddy
description:
## Question

food.davewil.dev exists in Cloudflare. Orange-cloud proxied or DNS-only? slackex uses caddy-docker-proxy with CF DNS-challenge Let's Encrypt certs either way. If proxied: CF terminates edge TLS, needs Full (strict) to origin, and LiveView websockets ride CF's WS proxying. If DNS-only: caddy is the only TLS terminator, origin IP is public. Decide the mode, the CF SSL setting, and confirm LiveView websockets work through the choice.
close_reason: Decided: orange-cloud proxied, zone SSL Full (strict), CF default websocket proxying. See resolution comment.

comments.author: davewil
comments.text:
RESOLUTION:

**Mode: orange-cloud proxied. Zone SSL: Full (strict). Websockets: CF defaults, no special config.**

- Verified: the whole davewil.dev zone is already proxied (slackex + food both resolve to CF edge IPs); food.davewil.dev's proxied A record already exists.
- Ingress: router port-forward 443 → caddy-docker-proxy on 192.168.1.102 (same as all other deployments; no cloudflared tunnel).
- TLS chain: browser → CF edge cert → TLS to origin → caddy, which holds a genuine Let's Encrypt cert via the CF DNS-01 challenge. Full (strict) is therefore free to satisfy — caddy's cert is publicly trusted.
- LiveView websockets: CF proxies WS upgrades on all plans; LiveView's 30s client heartbeat keeps sockets under CF's ~100s idle timeout. Caddy passes upgrades natively. No timeout tuning, no extra labels.
- App-side: check_origin/PHX_HOST=food.davewil.dev — belongs to the compose/deploy work (tdu.4/tdu.6), not this decision.
- Follow-through for the smoke checklist (fog item on the map): verify via CF API (not dashboard) that (1) zone SSL mode is Full (strict), (2) zone WebSockets setting is enabled — slackex may not exercise WS so the toggle is unproven on this zone.

title: Research: pick the prod Postgres image (pgvector-pg16 vs the PG18+ claim)
description:
## Question

Dev compose runs pgvector/pgvector:pg16, but CLAUDE.md claims PostgreSQL 18+ is required. Which is true, and what image tag should prod use? Establish: (1) what the app actually requires — ash-functions, citext, AshMoney extension, pgvector — and whether any of it needs PG18; (2) whether a pgvector PG17/PG18 image exists and is stable; (3) the recommended image tag for docker-compose.prod.yml. AFK research; answer is a recommendation with evidence.
close_reason: research complete: use pgvector/pgvector:pg18 (0.8.5, amd64) for prod; PG18+ claim is installer drift, actual floor is PG16 (CI-proven) - evidence in docs/research/prod-postgres-image.md on research/prod-postgres-image

comments.author: davewil
comments.text:
RESOLUTION: Use pgvector/pgvector:pg18 (pgvector 0.8.5, multi-arch amd64+arm64, pushed 2026-07-08) in docker-compose.prod.yml; pin by digest at deploy time.

Verdict on the PG18+ claim: it is documentation drift, not a real requirement. min_pg_version 18 in lib/grocery_planner/repo.ex was auto-detected by the ash_postgres installer running 'postgres -V' on the dev machine at project generation; CLAUDE.md echoed it as a hard requirement. The actual floor is PG16: ash_postgres 2.6.25's only min_pg_version-gated SQL is any_value (a PG16 feature), all migrations use gen_random_uuid()/a uuid_generate_v7 polyfill (no native uuidv7 dependency), and CI runs the full suite on pgvector/pgvector:pg16. ash_postgres officially supports PG 14+.

Why pg18 anyway: fresh DB (no upgrade path), PG18 is stable GA (18.0 2025-09-25, current 18.4, EOL 2030-11-14), official pgvector pg18 images exist, and it makes the declared min_pg_version 18 accurate. Fallback pg17/pg16 works without code changes. pgvector IS required at runtime (recipes.embedding vector(384), HNSW index, semantic search) - a plain postgres image will not work.

Full evidence: docs/research/prod-postgres-image.md on branch research/prod-postgres-image (pushed to origin). Follow-ups noted there: bump dev/CI to pg18, fix CLAUDE.md wording.

title: Wayfinder map: production-ready on the homelab for household daily use
description:
## Destination

grocery_planner live on the homelab Docker host (192.168.1.102) at food.davewil.dev — public via Cloudflare, caddy-proxied, deployed by the slackex pattern (GH Actions → GHCR → Tailscale SSH → pull/migrate/recreate). Fresh database; CPU Python sidecar alongside for v1 (Tesseract OCR, MiniLM embeddings on CPU — GPU/ROCm is viable on the headless Proxmox host but deferred past v1, see Notes + grocery_planner-89l); household accounts seeded via console; deploy-blocking hardening done; the three data-corrupting P1 bugs fixed; Fly config removed from the repo (the dormant Fly app itself untouched).

## Notes

- Execution is carried into this map (owner's call): the map ends with a running system, not a spec.
- Deployment exemplar: slackex at /root/slackex/ on the Docker host — GHCR image, docker-compose.prod.yml + .env per app dir, caddy-docker-proxy labels, postgres on implicit default network, app on default + proxy networks.
- Org-brain cards that apply: provision-homelab-ci-deploy-secrets, homelab-ci-tailnet-before-ssh, single-instance-ship-ready-defer-liveness, test-behaviour-at-the-boundary-not-implementation.
- Skills to consult per ticket type: /grilling, /domain-modeling, /prototype, /research.
- GPU for AI/ML on the homelab is VIABLE, not banned (corrected 2026-07-14): the old "no GPU, ever" was ROCm↔Wayland-compositor contention on the bare-metal Omarchy install, which cannot occur on the headless Proxmox host — both Vulkan and ROCm are stable headless (org-brain: [[headless-amd-apu-blacklist-amdgpu-for-cpu-only-ml]], [[which-local-ml-workloads-suit-a-680m-uma-igpu]]). The 680M is a modest UMA iGPU: a portfolio/demo-scale enabler for inference/prefill/embeddings/DL-OCR (production GPU grunt = cloud/VPS). v1 still ships CPU (Tesseract is CPU-only regardless; MiniLM is fine on CPU; ROCm torch is a ~30GB image). GPU-accelerated AI/ML is captured as a deferred option — see Out of scope + grocery_planner-89l.
- In-scope pre-existing beads issues are linked as children of this map: cxk, vzc, 83o (data bugs); 4p7, 5dy, yh3, nhu (hardening); t7j (OTel — homelab collector already provisioned); c29 (health/ready endpoint).

## Decisions so far

- Destination + scope grilling (this session): homelab target, public via Cloudflare at food.davewil.dev; fresh DB (nothing real on Fly); sidecar deploys CPU-only; feature cut-line = no new features; 83o added to bug scope; backups OUT (owner's explicit call); no outbound email in v1 (accounts by seed/console); OTel IN — wire to existing homelab stack; Fly config removed from repo only.
- [Research: pick the prod Postgres image (grocery_planner-tdu.1)](closed) — use pgvector/pgvector:pg18 (pgvector 0.8.5); the "PG18+ required" claim was installer drift from `postgres -V` autodetect, real floor is PG16, but fresh DB → newest stable. Detail: docs/research/prod-postgres-image.md on branch research/prod-postgres-image.
- [Decide: Cloudflare proxy mode and the TLS chain to caddy (grocery_planner-tdu.2)](closed) — orange-cloud proxied, zone SSL Full (strict); chain: browser → CF edge → TLS to origin (router 443 port-forward, no tunnel) → caddy holding a real LE cert via DNS-01. LiveView websockets ride CF's default WS proxying (30s heartbeat < ~100s idle timeout), no special config. check_origin/PHX_HOST land in tdu.4/tdu.6.
- [Decide: force_ssl/HSTS yes — but does Postgres TLS survive scoping? (grocery_planner-tdu.3)](closed) — force_ssl: [rewrite_on: [:x_forwarded_proto], hsts: true] (rewrite_on mandatory: caddy terminates TLS, app sees plain HTTP); no subdomains/preload. Postgres TLS DROPPED (single-host docker network, no untrusted hop); 4p7 amended to force_ssl/HSTS only.
- [Prototype: docker-compose.prod.yml + caddy labels for the three-service stack (grocery_planner-tdu.4)](closed) — draft on branch prototype/prod-compose (promoted to main by tdu.6). HF model cache = named volume (not baked); sidecar ceiling 2cpu/2g (host LXC has 12 CPUs); flags: receipts+search ON, AI categorization OFF pending DeepInfra review (grocery_planner-uox, out of map scope); TIDEWAVE_ENABLED pinned false; sidecar optional at boot (service_started + required:false). Runner image needs curl (noted on c29); two one-time volume chowns + migrate cmd in compose header.
- [Task: provision the three CI deploy secrets (grocery_planner-tdu.5)](closed) — all three GH repo secrets set on d-j-will/grocery_planner: `DEPLOY_HOST`=100.109.69.108 (docker-host tailnet IP), `DEPLOY_SSH_KEY` (dedicated ed25519 `deploy@github-actions` keypair, pubkey installed on docker-host authorized_keys + verified authenticating over LAN, private half lives only in the secret), `TAILSCALE_AUTHKEY` (reusable/ephemeral/pre-approved, minted by owner). Dual-secret deploy gate (per org-brain provision-homelab-ci-deploy-secrets) now satisfiable in tdu.6. FLAG carried to tdu.6/cutover: this workstation (tailnet node) could NOT reach host 100.109.69.108:22 though LAN works → tailnet ACLs likely gate host access; the CI ephemeral runner needs `tag:ci` + a matching ACL rule (and creator in `tagOwners`) to route, else the deploy hangs at SSH.
- [Task: reclaim disk on the docker host (grocery_planner-86p)](closed) — owner pruned docker-host: / now at 65% (was 83%), 11G free of 32G — comfortable for the ~2-3GB first deploy. (docker's 11.28GB "reclaimable" is 27/28 ACTIVE images from other running apps, not real headroom.) Last of tdu.6's three blockers cleared → **tdu.6 (deploy workflow) is now the open frontier.**

## Not yet specified

- Cutover / definition-of-live smoke checklist — sharpens once the deploy workflow and compose exist. Now includes two CF API verifications from tdu.2: zone SSL mode is Full (strict), zone WebSockets setting enabled (slackex may not exercise WS, so unproven). Optional third from tdu.3: CF Always-Use-HTTPS toggle. From tdu.5: verify the CI ephemeral runner can actually SSH the host over the tailnet (tailnet ACL/tag gating suspected — a non-tagged tailnet node was blocked from host:22).
- Ops runbook niggles (log access, restart, migration-failure recovery) — shape emerges from the first real deploy. From tdu.4: two one-time volume chowns (uploads→65534, hf-cache→1000) and the compose-run migrate command, both documented in the prototype compose header.

## Out of scope

- Backups — owner explicitly deferred; revisit after daily use begins.
- Outbound email (confirmation, password reset, invitations) — accounts seeded via console; reset = console.
- All new features, Tonight screen included — post-migration backlog.
- Anything on Fly.io itself — app stays dormant; only repo config is removed.
- GPU-accelerated AI/ML (vLLM/vision-OCR, GPU embeddings) — VIABLE on the headless Proxmox host, not banned (org-brain: [[headless-amd-apu-blacklist-amdgpu-for-cpu-only-ml]]); deferred past v1 as a portfolio/demo enabler, captured in grocery_planner-89l. v1 sidecar runs CPU; the vLLM OCR path stays flagged off for v1.
- Observability stack changes — collector/backend already provisioned on the homelab.
- DeepInfra-backed categorization — post-migration backlog (grocery_planner-uox); categorization OFF is the v1 posture (v1 sidecar runs CPU). GPU-local categorization on the homelab is now also an option (grocery_planner-89l), not only the DeepInfra route.

title: Focus mode copy_previous_day never works on Mondays (week-cache miss)
description: focus_layout.ex copy_previous_day read source-day meals from socket.assigns.week_meals, which only holds the displayed week. When selected_day is the first day of the week (every Monday), yesterday belongs to the previous week, the cache lookup returns nothing, and the user gets 'No meals found on previous day' even when Sunday had meals. Side effect: the date-dependent test 'copies yesterday's meals to today' failed every Monday UTC, blocking all CI merges that day. Fixed by querying list_meal_plans_by_date_range for the source day instead of the cache, plus a deterministic regression test that pins the Monday case by selecting the week's first day explicitly.
close_reason: Fixed in same PR: copy_previous_day now queries list_meal_plans_by_date_range for the source day instead of the week-scoped cache; deterministic regression test pins the Monday/week-boundary case. Was also blocking all Monday-UTC CI runs via the date-dependent test.

title: Data portability: full export + Mealie/Tandoor/Paprika importers
description: Yummly died Dec 2024 with no bulk export - users lost everything; PlateJoy followed in 2025. Portability is now the loudest category grievance and a self-hosted app should own it on principle: one-click full export (recipes, plans, inventory, preferences) in open formats (JSON + schema.org recipes), and importers for Mealie/Tandoor/Paprika archives - which is simultaneously the adoption funnel FROM those apps. Cheap, principled, marketable.

title: Leftovers as first-class inventory entries
description: Leftovers are one of the biggest waste contributors and invisible to nearly every planner - we are halfway there with leftover chains (base -> follow-up recipes, usage_type :leftover). Close the loop: completing a meal with extra servings creates a leftovers inventory entry (short expiry, fridge location) that shows in eat-me-first, satisfies follow-up-recipe ingredients, and can be planned directly as tomorrow's lunch (eat-leftovers slot type). Only niche apps (Eat This Much, SideChef cook-once-eat-twice) touch this.
notes: Evidence upgrade from final competitor deep-dive: cooked-leftover INVENTORY (portions + cooked-on/use-by) is near-total whitespace ranked #2 best-evidenced opportunity. Demand is GitHub-validated (Grocy #942 closed unimplemented, #1181 portion-scaling broken; Samsung Food review: cannot track leftovers; 2025 food-waste roundup names the gap). Scheduling a leftover NIGHT is solved (Plan to Eat); the cooked-dish-as-dated-portioned-inventory half is the gap we should own. Raised P2->P1.

title: Meal-plan templates UI (backend fully built, zero UI)
description: MealPlanTemplate + entries are fully modelled server-side with CRUD, activate/deactivate, and sync/JSON-API exposure - but no screen creates, names, or applies one. Cheapest feature on this list: save-current-week-as-template, apply-template-to-week (respecting occupied slots), template library. Solves the rigidity pain point (rotating known-good weeks beats replanning from scratch) and un-strands sync-ready backend work.

title: Aisle/section-ordered shopping lists per store
description: Now a baseline expectation - AnyList, Mealime, Plan to Eat, Tandoor, even Apple Grocery sort by store section; reviewers describe unsorted lists as broken (crisscrossing the store turns 20 minutes into an hour). Category-to-section mapping per named store (our categories already exist), drag-to-reorder sections to match the local shop, group same-ingredient lines (all cheeses together). Optionally learn order from check-off sequence over time.

title: Personal item price history from receipts
description: The genuine market gap: comparison sites (Trolley.co.uk) track shelf prices, but nobody shows what YOUR household actually paid over time - and we already capture the raw material in receipt line items with prices and dates. Per-item price timeline (you paid 1.10 -> 1.35 -> 1.60 over 8 months), sparkline on the item page, per-store comparison from receipt merchants. Private-by-construction - a cloud app cannot credibly offer this. AnyList-style price tracking is one of the few features users pay for. Depends on the receipt pipeline being robust (review finding cxk).

title: Finish email invitations: invite non-registered members to the household
description: The invite flow only attaches already-registered users by email lookup; inviting an actual new person (send email -> they sign up -> auto-join household with chosen role) is unbuilt. Family participation is pain-point number 8 - the enthusiast adopts, the household does not - and this is the literal front gate. Low effort, completes an existing half-feature, prerequisite for every household-coordination idea below.

title: Tonight screen: expiry- and family-aware dinner suggestions as the front door
description: The number-one job in every pain-point source is what is for dinner tonight with what I have - decision fatigue kills weekly-grid planners. We already have all the pieces buried inside the meal planner (waste-watch, cook-with-these, MealOptimizer scoring, family preference set-cover); elevate them into the default landing experience: 3 suggestions ranked by expiring inventory x on-hand coverage x family compatibility, one tap to plan or start cooking, an eat-me-first strip of items to use up. Optional daily push/email at 4pm. Turns the app from a planning tool into a daily answer machine.

title: Frictionless pantry mode: on-hand flags, loose quantities, auto-decrement on cooked meals
description: Inventory upkeep is the category-killer: Grocy drowns users in data entry (reviewers: a very demanding data entry job), Mealie refuses to build pantry tracking at all. The winning wedge is inventory as a passive byproduct: half-a-pack granularity instead of exact counts, an on-hand toggle per item, auto-decrement of ingredients when a planned meal is marked completed, and fully wiring the existing fuzzy ItemMatcher into receipt confirmation (currently exact-name only). We already own the hard part (receipt OCR); this makes the data trustworthy without discipline.

title: Recipe import from any URL and social video (with embedding dedupe)
description: Table stakes everywhere (Paprika, Mealie, Tandoor, Samsung Food all have it) yet we import only from TheMealDB - the single biggest feature gap vs the whole category. Recipes actually live in Instagram/TikTok/YouTube/blogs/photos; imports that work there are the most-shipped, most-loved AI food feature of 2025-26 (Mealie ships video transcription -> LLM extraction). Our edge: dedupe and cross-link imports against existing pgvector embeddings so the household corpus stays clean (nobody else does import-dedupe). Pipeline: schema.org scraper first, AI-sidecar LLM extraction fallback for video/social/photo.

title: Add family_live LiveView tests (FM-002 has zero UI coverage)
description: family_live.ex (294 lines, the FM-002 meal-time-solution feature from the latest commits) has no LiveView test at all - only domain-level unit tests exist. The suite already has the right pattern (mount -> interact -> assert, see receipt_live_test.exs). Cover: mount renders members, click-to-exclude member in meal time solution, preference toggling, 2-state preference model rendering.
acceptance_criteria:
- test/grocery_planner_web/live/family_live_test.exs exercises mount + exclusion click + preference toggle via render_click
- Uses existing FamilyTestHelpers (production code paths)

title: Configure prod OpenTelemetry exporter (currently exporting to nowhere)
description: application.ex:12 calls setup_opentelemetry() unconditionally, but an exporter endpoint is configured only in config/dev.exs:96-98 (set to :none). In prod the exporter falls back to localhost:4317 and every span is silently dropped - full instrumentation cost, zero visibility. docker-compose.yml:24 sets OTEL_ENABLED only on the python service; the Elixir app reads nothing. Add config :opentelemetry_exporter, otlp_endpoint: System.get_env(...) under prod in runtime.exs and gate setup_opentelemetry() on OTEL_ENABLED so it no-ops without a collector.
acceptance_criteria:
- Prod exporter endpoint env-configurable; setup gated on OTEL_ENABLED
- With no collector configured, OTel setup no-ops (no background export errors)
- With a collector configured, spans arrive (verified against homelab collector or documented as pending one)
close_reason: Completed in 539a6e3 (was left in_progress). runtime.exs gates prod OTel on OTEL_ENABLED + OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT (unset->off/:none; enabled-no-endpoint->warns+disables; enabled+endpoint->:otlp to it); setup_opentelemetry/0 skips attaching handlers when disabled. All 3 acceptance criteria met — criterion 3 (spans arrive) documented as pending a homelab collector (INFRA-002), which the criterion allows. Covered by test/config/runtime_otel_test.exs (3 branches) + telemetry_test.exs. CI green.

title: Prevent duplicate meal-plan slots: unique index + occupied-slot handling on all add paths
description: MealPlan has no identity/unique index on (account_id, scheduled_date, meal_type). perform_add_meal (meal_planner_live.ex:1490 area), drop_recipe (:553), and copy_last_week all create without checking slot occupancy, and data_loader.ex:41 masks duplicates via Enum.into keyed on meal_type - hidden orphan rows accumulate. Add an Ash identity (partial unique index respecting deleted_at soft-deletes), handle the conflict in the create paths (replace or reject), and make data_loader surface rather than mask.
acceptance_criteria:
- Migration adds partial unique index on (account_id, scheduled_date, meal_type) where deleted_at is null
- Adding to an occupied slot has defined behavior (replace or friendly error) on every path incl copy_last_week (tests)
- Data cleanup for existing duplicate rows handled in the migration or a task
notes: Design decisions (user-approved): interactive occupied-slot = REPLACE (upsert :place); undo-restore = restore-and-replace; bulk copy = SKIP occupied. Replace is upsert-overwrite-in-place (no tombstone), so undo-of-a-replace leaves the slot empty rather than restoring the clobbered meal. Swap uses a deleted_at-hide/move/unhide transaction because the immediate partial unique index is checked per-row (single-statement swap fails). Migration dedup keeps newest per slot (lossy across differing recipes).
close_reason: Shipped: partial unique slot index + :place upsert (replace) + bulk pre-filter (skip) + transactional swap + dedup migration + data_loader collision warning. 8 boundary tests green; full precommit green (932 tests).

title: Serve receipt images through authenticated tenant-scoped controller instead of Plug.Static
description: Receipt images (purchase PII) are written to priv/static/uploads/receipts/ (receipt_processor.ex:44-61) and served by Plug.Static via the uploads entry in static_paths (grocery_planner_web.ex:20) with no auth or tenant check. UUID filenames make guessing impractical but any leaked URL exposes another tenant receipt to anyone. Store outside priv/static and serve via a controller that verifies the actor belongs to the receipt account; re-validate extension server-side in store_file/1 (currently client-derived Path.extname).
acceptance_criteria:
- Unauthenticated request for a receipt image gets 401/403/404
- Authenticated user of a different account gets 403/404 (test)
- Files stored outside priv/static; upload extension validated server-side
- Existing receipt display flows still work

title: API bearer tokens: add revocation - survive logout and password reset for 24h
description: plugs/api_auth.ex:16 verifies Phoenix.Token with max_age 86_400 and payload of just user.id. No server-side revocation: a stolen token stays valid through logout AND password reset (api/auth_controller.ex:9 mints; reset flow rotates nothing). Options: embed a per-user token_version checked on verify and bumped on logout/password-reset, or a server-side token store; also consider shortening max_age.
acceptance_criteria:
- Password reset invalidates previously issued API tokens (test)
- Logout (or an explicit revoke endpoint) invalidates tokens (test)
- Decision on max_age recorded

title: Enable force_ssl/HSTS in production (Postgres TLS dropped by tdu.3)
description:
No force_ssl anywhere (config/runtime.exs:110-116 has it only in comments). Session cookie and API bearer tokens are capturable via downgrade/MITM on first visit. Add to the prod endpoint:

    force_ssl: [rewrite_on: [:x_forwarded_proto], hsts: true]

rewrite_on is mandatory: per wayfinder decision tdu.2 the app never terminates TLS (CF edge → caddy TLS → app over plain HTTP on the docker proxy network), so Plug.SSL must trust caddy's X-Forwarded-Proto header or the site redirect-loops. No subdomains: true, no preload (per tdu.3).

Postgres TLS was DROPPED from this ticket's scope by wayfinder decision tdu.3: app ↔ postgres share the single-host compose default network — no untrusted hop; postgres image ships no certs so TLS would mean cert mint/mount/rotate for ~zero threat coverage. Repo ssl stays commented out (runtime.exs:45). Revisit only if the DB moves off-host.
acceptance_criteria:
- Prod endpoint sets force_ssl: [rewrite_on: [:x_forwarded_proto], hsts: true]
- No Repo ssl change (explicitly out of scope per tdu.3)
- Deploy verified: https serves without redirect loops, HSTS header present on responses

title: Deduplicate receipt-item-to-inventory logic: price field drift and hardcoded USD
description: The create-inventory-entry-from-receipt-item logic is duplicated and has drifted: receipts_live.ex:740-772 uses item.unit_price for purchase_price while receipt_processor.ex:351-379 uses item.total_price - the same receipt imported via /receipts vs /receipts/scan records different prices. parse_flat_money (receipt_processor.ex:403-424) also hardcodes :USD (app default is :GBP) and builds Money from raw JSON floats. Extract one shared function with one price policy and currency from config.
acceptance_criteria:
- Single shared function used by both paths
- Explicit decision on unit_price vs total_price recorded in the code
- Currency comes from app config, not a literal
- Test covering both import paths producing identical entries

title: Add anti-corruption layer for AI sidecar responses (categorizer + embeddings) and resolve dead Contracts module
description: ai/categorizer.ex trusts Python-service JSON raw with concrete crash vectors: String.to_existing_atom on server strings (230, 245), is_float-guarded confidence fed JSON ints/nulls (190-224), single vs batch paths derive confidence_level differently; embeddings.ex:31-46 has no clause for empty embeddings list. Meanwhile contracts.ex - built to validate exactly this boundary - is never called anywhere (dead code). Also fold in: ai_client.ex:152 ignores the :ai_service_url runtime config and re-reads env directly (runtime.exs:76-78 is dead config), and ai_client.ex:188-190 logs full response bodies (receipt PII) at error level. Model the normalizer on the_meal_db.ex parse_meal/1.
acceptance_criteria:
- Normalizing parser validates category, coerces confidence to float with default, maps confidence_level defensively; malformed payloads degrade with error tuples, never raise (tests with int/null/unknown inputs)
- Contracts module either wired in or deleted
- ai_client reads Application.get_env(:grocery_planner, :ai_service_url)
- Error logs no longer include full response bodies
close_reason: Never-raise normalizing ACL for categorizer (coerce confidence, derive confidence_level, validate category, error tuples) + embeddings empty-list clause. Deleted dead+unsafe Contracts/AI.Schema (atomize ran String.to_existing_atom on every server key). ai_client reads :ai_service_url app config; error log drops PII body. Boundary tests: int/null/unknown/extra-key/empty. precommit green 925 tests.

title: Wire recipe embedding AshOban trigger and delete raw-SQL workaround workers
description: The declarative ash_ai_update_embeddings trigger (recipes/recipe.ex:88) never fires: application.ex:18 hands AshOban only [Inventory] while config.exs:84 lists [Inventory, Recipes]. Recipes are never auto-embedded; semantic search silently degrades until manual backfill. The raw-SQL workers (workers/embedding_worker.ex:22-33, embedding_backfill_worker.ex) exist only as a workaround, bypass Ash/tenancy/field-contract, and have zero tests. Add Recipes to AshOban.config in application.ex, verify the trigger enqueues on create/update, then remove the manual workers (or rewrite any remaining manual path through the Recipe resource with authorize?: false + tenant).
acceptance_criteria:
- Creating/updating a recipe enqueues an embedding job (test)
- Raw-SQL workers deleted or rewritten through Ash
- Backfill path still available and tested
- application.ex and config.exs domain lists agree

title: Receipt worker robustness: stuck Processing on failure, duplicate items on retry, premature completed status
description: Three related defects in receipt_processor.ex under the AshOban :process trigger: (1) line 112 hard-matches {:ok, _} = update_receipt - any validation failure raises, no receipt_failed broadcast, UI spins on Processing forever; (2) lines 131-134 create items with no idempotency - Oban retry after partial success duplicates line items; (3) status flips to :completed before items persist, so a mid-run crash leaves a completed receipt with partial items (98-145). Feature is flag-gated off today; fix before flipping receipt_processing on.
acceptance_criteria:
- Update failures produce a failed status + receipt_failed broadcast (test)
- Item creation idempotent under retry: re-running the worker yields no duplicates (test)
- Status set to completed only after items persist
- check_duplicate (line 90) fails closed on query error

title: Receipt pipeline error-rate metric split by error class (:unavailable / :bad_input / :transient)
description:
AI-006 §7 lists 'error rate split by class' as a load-bearing metric — it maps onto §4b's decision table (does the failure mean fix the sidecar or fix the input). NOT covered by Arc 3 (4x6.4): PromEx's Oban plugin gives error COUNTS by worker/queue via oban_queue_length_count and job-event metrics, but not split by our domain error classification.

Needs: emit a :telemetry event (or PromEx counter) tagged with the classified error class when a pipeline worker fails, so Prometheus can break error rate down by :unavailable vs {:bad_input,_} vs {:transient,_}. Depends on Arc 1's error classification (already landed in dc2aff9). Wire it in the workers' error/rescue path in GroceryPlanner.Inventory.Receipts.*Worker.

Not in 4x6.4's acceptance gate (which names awaiting_ai + discarded + Oban Web + spans), so split out to keep Arc 3 shippable.
acceptance_criteria: Prometheus can query receipt worker error rate broken down by error class label

title: Wire grocery_planner into the existing homelab observability stack (Prometheus scrape + Grafana + OTLP traces)
description:
Arc 3 (4x6.4) lands the app-side code: PromEx exposes GET /metrics, Oban Web dashboard, and the awaiting_ai/discarded domain metrics. This issue is the DEPLOYMENT wiring to make them alertable against the stack that already runs on the docker-host LXC (built for slackex).

Findings from homelab inspection (2026-07-16):
- Existing stack on 192.168.1.102: slackex-otel-collector-1 (OTLP 4317/4318 published on host), slackex-prometheus-1 (prom/prometheus:v3.12, only on slackex_default), slackex-grafana-1 (grafana 13, host port 3002), slackex-tempo-1 (traces).
- NETWORK GAP: slackex-prometheus-1 is only on slackex_default; grocery_planner-app-1 is on grocery_planner_default + proxy. They share NO network, so Prometheus cannot currently scrape grocery_planner. Fix: attach Prometheus to a shared network (proxy, or a dedicated 'monitoring' external net) and add a scrape_config job targeting grocery_planner-app-1:4000 /metrics (mirror slackex/infra/prometheus.yml app1/app2 pattern).
- TRACES: set OTEL_ENABLED=true + OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT=http://slackex-otel-collector-1:4317 (or host-ip:4317) in grocery_planner/.env; t7j already gates on these. Needs the app on a net that reaches the collector (or use published host ports).
- DASHBOARDS: import PromEx-generated Grafana dashboards (Application/Beam/Phoenix/Ecto/Oban) + a receipt-pipeline dashboard into slackex-grafana. Consider whether the stack should graduate from slackex-scoped to a shared 'observability' compose (decision note).

Acceptance: Prometheus scrapes grocery_planner /metrics; awaiting_ai + discarded are queryable/alertable in Prometheus; a web request traces through into an Oban worker in Tempo; Grafana shows the dashboards.
acceptance_criteria: Prometheus scrapes grocery_planner /metrics; awaiting_ai + discarded alertable; trace flows web->worker in Tempo; Grafana dashboards imported
notes: SECURITY: /metrics is served unauthenticated on the app port (mirrors slackex). caddy currently proxies the whole app publicly, so food.davewil.dev/metrics would be world-readable (aggregate ops data: queue depths, receipt counts, app version/git sha — no PII/receipt contents). This wiring MUST block /metrics at caddy so it is only scrapeable internally on the docker network. Prefer the caddy-level block over app-level token auth (config-as-IaC, and Prometheus scrapes the container directly on the shared network anyway).

title: Repeatable local Python test runner + bump Python 3.11→3.12
description: The Python sidecar suite only ran in CI (no local venv; system python is 3.9). Add a uv-based Makefile in python_service so pytest/ruff/uvicorn run locally with one command (shift-left to the commit boundary). Bump Python 3.11.15→3.12 across .tool-versions (CI reads it) and the Dockerfile (off aging Debian bullseye to bookworm). Verify full stack + pytest green on 3.12 locally and in CI.
close_reason: Shipped: python_service/Makefile (uv runner, make test/lint/serve, pins Python from .tool-versions). Bumped 3.11.15→3.12.13 in .tool-versions + Dockerfile (bullseye→bookworm). Verified local (89 passed/1 skip, ruff clean) and CI green (0e040c2).

title: AI-006 Arc 3: observability — land the exporter, Oban Web, and the outage signal
description:
Arc 3 of AI-006 (specs/AI-006-receipt-pipeline-rework.md §7). No dependency on Arcs 1/2 — can land first or in parallel. Landing it BEFORE Arc 2 makes the rework measurable rather than asserted (real before/after on stuck receipts and duplicate items).

Smaller than it looks: the instrumentation is ALREADY wired and correct. application.ex:47-52 calls OpentelemetryPhoenix.setup(), OpentelemetryEcto.setup() and OpentelemetryOban.setup(). opentelemetry_oban (1.1.1) propagates trace context into workers automatically - injects traceparent into job meta on insert, extracts on execution - so the staged pipeline inherits tracing for free. Elixir->Python propagation is already correct too (ai_client.ex:178 passes propagate_trace_ctx: true, and telemetry.py instruments FastAPI to extract it).

It is producing spans and throwing every one away for want of an exporter. That is t7j.

DO NOT 'fix' ai_client.ex:178 to propagate_trace_headers. That option does not exist in the installed opentelemetry_req; register_options accepts only [:span_name, :no_path_params, :propagate_trace_ctx] and Req raises on unknown options. (A research pass advised this, citing docs for a version we don't run.)
design:
1. t7j (dependency): configure the OTLP endpoint in runtime.exs under prod; gate setup_opentelemetry() on OTEL_ENABLED so it no-ops without a collector. docker-compose.yml:24 sets OTEL_ENABLED on the python service only - the Elixir app reads nothing.
2. Add {:oban_web, ...} - not currently a dep. Free/Apache-2.0 since 2025-01-16 (previously commercial; the memory of it being paid is out of date). Gives discarded-job visibility for the cost of one dep.
3. Metrics, in priority order:
   - count(receipts where condition == :awaiting_ai) — THE OUTAGE SIGNAL. Domain metric; more meaningful than queue depth; the thing that stops §4c's snooze from hiding an outage. Not optional if we snooze.
   - discarded/cancelled job counts — dead-letter signal, alert on non-zero.
   - queue depth (available + scheduled + retryable) per queue.
   - error rate SPLIT BY CLASS (:unavailable / {:bad_input,_} / {:transient,_}) — load-bearing, not cosmetic: maps onto §4b's decision table, so it says whether to fix the sidecar or the input. Depends on Arc 1's classification.
   - sidecar saturation — CPU-bound at cpus: 2, saturates long before Oban. OOM-kill won't look like an application error.
4. Dashboards: INFRA-002 already specs the Grafana stack. Build on it; don't redesign it.
acceptance_criteria: With no collector configured, OTel setup no-ops with no background export errors; with one configured, spans arrive and a web request traces through into the Oban worker; Oban Web reachable; awaiting_ai count and discarded count are alertable

title: AI-006 Arc 4: collapse the sidecar to stateless — delete the dead paths
description:
Arc 4 of AI-006. Subtraction only; goes last. Depends on Arcs 1+2.

Oban owns job state, so the sidecar becomes a stateless synchronous function over base64, rebuildable from its Dockerfile with nothing to lose.

DELETE: jobs.py + ai_jobs table + /api/v1/jobs + the receipt_extraction handler (it branches on USE_VLLM_OCR -> else mock, and prod runs Tesseract, so adopting it would ship 'Mock Supermarket' to users); /api/v1/receipts/extract + its rich schemas (needs image_path; ai-service doesn't mount receipt-uploads, so it cannot work in prod); ai_feedback + /api/v1/feedback (dead - feedback lives in Postgres via item_handlers.ex:296); _executor = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=4) (declared, never referenced).

ai_artifacts: retire rather than fix. It is write-only (no Elixir client fn; ADR 4 gives the service no ingress, so it's readable only via docker exec), unbounded (the promised 30-day prune was never implemented), and stores input_payload - which for extract-receipt is the full base64 image - into Column(Text). Arc 2's receipt.raw_extraction replaces it: tenant-scoped, backed up, joinable, prunable with the receipt.

Then ask whether SQLite is needed at all. Test: if this vanished on restart, would we lose anything we care about? If it's only model cache, that's files on a volume and AI_DATABASE_URL goes away.
design:
Subtraction only. Ordered — step 6 depends on step 1. Spec §6.

1. Retire ai_artifacts (needs Arc 2's receipt.raw_extraction). Not 'fix its retention' — retire. Input and output both stay recoverable without it: the image is already on disk, the extraction lands in raw_extraction (tenant-scoped, backed up, joinable, prunable with the receipt).
2. Delete ai_jobs + jobs.py + /api/v1/jobs + /api/v1/jobs/{id} + the receipt_extraction handler (main.py:1130 — branches USE_VLLM_OCR else MOCK; prod runs Tesseract, so this path serves 'Mock Supermarket' to real users) + AiClient.submit_job/get_job.
3. Delete ai_feedback + /api/v1/feedback. Dead: feedback lives in Postgres (ai_categorization_feedback, item_handlers.ex:296), which is correct — a user correcting a category is a domain event, not ML telemetry.
4. Delete /api/v1/receipts/extract + ReceiptExtractRequest/ReceiptExtractResponse/ExtractionResult/LineItem/MoneyInfo/MerchantInfo/DateInfo schemas (keep whatever receipt_ocr.py needs internally). It takes image_path; ai-service does not mount receipt-uploads, so it cannot work in this deployment.
5. Delete _executor (jobs.py:22, declared and never referenced).
6. Drop the datastore: database.py, the SQLAlchemy dep, AI_DATABASE_URL, the ai-data volume, the 12 Depends(get_db) params. Keep hf-cache (model files, disposable by definition).

TRAP: /health/ready (main.py:170) checks DB connectivity and IS the endpoint the Elixir health check calls. Step 6 must land WITH the health-check change, not after it, or readiness becomes a probe of something deleted. Interacts with c29.

Test: deletion is the test — the suite stays green with the tables and endpoints gone. Anything that breaks was testing something no production path used. Plus a live /health/ready check after the datastore goes.
acceptance_criteria: One extraction path remains; no unbounded base64 store; sidecar holds no state we'd miss on restart

title: Deepen recipe browsing: one DB-side Recipes.browse_recipes/2 read action
description:
Architecture-review deepening candidate #2 (grilled to shared understanding 2026-07-14).

PROBLEM: The recipe read action is too shallow to use — it takes no filter args, so
7 LiveViews pull ALL recipes via list_recipes_sorted and re-implement search/difficulty/
prep-time/cuisine/dietary/sort/paginate in-memory. Same String.contains?(downcase...) in
7 files; prep-time buckets + difficulty rank live in web defps (no locality, untestable
without a socket). recipes_live.ex:53 also wraps toggle_favorite in try/rescue over raw
{:ok,_} tuples — a direct CLAUDE.md rule violation.

SEAM (deletion test passes): one DB-side Ash `read :browse` action on Recipe replaces the
load-all-then-filter pipeline. All filter fields are real DB columns or the total_time_minutes
expression calc, so filtering/sorting/paginating all run in Postgres.
design:
Locked decisions (grilling 2026-07-14):
- Q1 SEAM: (A) DB-side Ash `read :browse` action, NOT an in-memory domain fn.
- Q2 PAGINATION: (A) optional offset pagination in the action (required?: false, countable: true).
  recipes_live passes page: -> Ash.Page.Offset (results+count); picker sites call w/o page: -> list.
- Q3 BREADTH: (B) union-minus-pantry. Eight optional args (nil/empty = no-op), NAMED TO MIRROR
  FilterPreset.criteria keys so saved presets round-trip:
    search (name ilike) | difficulty (==) | prep_time :quick<=30/:medium 31-60/:long>60 (over
    total_time_minutes calc) | favorites (is_favorite==true) | chains (is_base_recipe or
    is_follow_up) | cuisine (ilike) | dietary_needs (array CONTAINS-ALL) | sort_by
    (name/newest/prep_time/difficulty).
- Q4 CADENCE: (A) incremental — ship action + migrate recipes_live FIRST (richest caller,
  exercises every arg + pagination), verify, then fan out.
- Q5 SCOPE: (A) fold the toggle_favorite try/rescue fix into the recipes_live commit
  (new Recipes.toggle_favorite action; :53 -> pipe).
- Q6 TESTS: (A) boundary-first. Domain code-interface tests: one case per argument + pagination
  page-2/count; toggle_favorite extends recipe_favorite_test.exs. Thin recipes_live tests to
  wiring/flow; delete socket-level filter re-assertions.
- Q7 NAME: (A) browse_recipes (action :browse). `search_recipes` is already taken by
  Embeddings.search_recipes/2 (semantic) and External.search_recipes/1 (importer). Add
  "Recipe browsing" term to CONTEXT.md (create lazily; distinct from semantic/external search).

DEFERRED (fast-follow, separate ticket): the explorer "pantry" sort (fewest ingredients, take 24)
— one call site, needs a recipe_ingredients count aggregate.

CARRIED FORWARD verbatim (not redesigned): 30/60 prep thresholds; dietary_needs = satisfy-ALL;
current default sort for a no-args browse.

FAN-OUT ORDER (follow-up commits): explorer_layout, family_live, recipe_show_live, power_layout,
focus_layout, meal_planner_live. LAST: delete now-dead list_recipes_sorted + favorites actions
(apply deletion test then).

The model to copy: Analytics is already the deep module — thin LiveView over a domain module
that owns aggregation. Make the recipe seam look like Analytics.
acceptance_criteria:
First commit (this ticket's DoD):
- [ ] Recipe has `read :browse` with the 8 optional args; all filtering/sorting via filter expr/sort in SQL (no Enum filtering of a full result set).
- [ ] Optional offset pagination (required?: false, countable: true); page: returns results + count.
- [ ] define :browse_recipes, action: :browse on the Recipes domain.
- [ ] recipes_live migrated: filter_by_*/sort_recipes/in-memory load_recipes deleted; handler assigns the page.
- [ ] Recipes.toggle_favorite action added; recipes_live.ex:53 try/rescue replaced with a pipe.
- [ ] Boundary tests: one domain case per argument (incl. each prep bucket, dietary contains-all, each sort) + pagination page-2/count; toggle_favorite covered in recipe_favorite_test.exs.
- [ ] recipes_live tests thinned to wiring/flow; no duplicated filter assertions through the socket.
- [ ] CONTEXT.md created with the "Recipe browsing" term.
- [ ] mix precommit green.
Fan-out + dead-action deletion tracked as follow-ups, NOT in this ticket's DoD.
notes:
== FAN-OUT RE-SCOPED after reading the call sites (2026-07-14) ==
The grilled plan (migrate all 6 + delete dead actions) was built on the explore
agents' "same String.contains in 7 files" COUNT — they didn't read access
patterns. Reading the code: only 1 of 6 sites is a real fit.

DO NOT MIGRATE (deliberate cache-then-filter-in-memory, NOT shallow load-all):
  - explorer_layout.load_explorer_recipes: filters socket.assigns.available_recipes (cached).
  - meal_planner_live search_recipes (:186) + focus_layout focus_search (:835): both use
    `all_recipes_cache if available, otherwise fetch` then filter the cached, association-loaded
    list in-memory per keystroke. Migrating -> per-keystroke DB query = REGRESSION + loses the
    list_recipes_for_meal_planner association loads.
  - family_live.filter_recipes: caches all_recipes because it ALSO needs the full list for the
    preference matrix; filters the cache in-memory.
  - power_layout: inline filter_fn over a cached list.
  Residual real duplication = only the search PREDICATE (and a nil-safety variance:
  some do String.downcase(name), others name || ""). A shared Recipes.name_matches?/2 would
  unify+fix it — tiny SEPARATE refactor, not "fan-out via browse_recipes". Offered, not folded in.

MIGRATE (genuine fit): recipe_show_live.load_linkable_recipes — re-query picker (load -> reject
  current/linked/follow-ups -> filter -> take 20). Replace filter_by_query with browse(%{search:}).
  Keep rejects + Enum.take(20) AFTER browse (do NOT push limit:20 into the query, or post-reject
  count drops below 20). Ordering shifts arbitrary-20 -> name-sorted-20 (deterministic; lock w/ test).

DEAD-ACTION DELETION = VOID: list_recipes_for_meal_planner, list_favorite_recipes, list_recipes,
  list_recipes_sorted are all still in legitimate use. Drop that step.

Net: "fan-out" collapses from 6 sites+deletion to 1 small site. First commit already captured the
real deepening.
close_reason: Deepening shipped: browse_recipes/2 (first commit fab-range) + fan-out re-scoped — recipe_show migrated, name_matches?/2 shared across the 5 cached sites, dead-action deletion void (all still in use). precommit green (996 tests). Pantry sort remains as uiy.

title: Quality-gate infra: bake in Credo + Dialyzer + lefthook gates, unified ci-deploy.yml (like predictex/slackex)
description:
Align grocery_planner's quality gates with the reference repos davewil/predictex and d-j-will/slackex. Separate from the deploy (tdu.6); the deploy only needs the 1.20 compile warnings fixed (in progress).

## Root cause this fixes
mix.exs precommit alias had a TYPO: 'compile --warning-as-errors' (singular) — so the LOCAL gate never enforced warnings-as-errors, warnings drifted, and only real CI (correct flag) caught them on the 1.20 bump. Fixing that + wiring the corrected gate into lefthook is what makes the whole effort stick. (Typo itself is being corrected in the deploy-unblock warning-fix pass.)

## Reference pattern (slackex mix.exs + ci-deploy.yml)
- mix.exs aliases: lint=[format --check-formatted, credo --strict, compile --warnings-as-errors]; typecheck=[dialyzer]; quality=[lint, typecheck, test]; ci=[deps.get, compile --warnings-as-errors, format --check-formatted, credo --strict, dialyzer, ecto setup, test].
- dialyzer config: plt_file {:no_warn, priv/plts/project.plt}, plt_add_apps [:mix, :ex_unit], flags [:unmatched_returns, :error_handling, :no_opaque], ignore_warnings '.dialyzer_ignore.exs'. Add {:dialyxir, ~> 1.4, only: [:dev,:test], runtime: false}.
- Unified .github/workflows/ci-deploy.yml: single 'quality' job (postgres service, setup-beam version-file, cache deps+build+PLTs, compile --warnings-as-errors, format check, credo --strict, dialyzer --format github, tests) gating the deploy leg. Replaces the current split ci.yml + deploy-homelab.yml.
- lefthook.yml: run 'mix precommit' (or lint) ABOVE the beads-export command in pre-commit (piped: true stops on failure before the Dolt sync). CC guard hooks already exist (.claude/hooks: block-amend, block-ecto, block-force-push-main, block-no-verify).

## CRITICAL caveats — 'exactly like slackex' does NOT fully transfer
1. DIALYZER MUST NOT be a blocking CI gate initially. grocery_planner is Ash-heavy and has NEVER been dialyzed; slackex's aggressive flags (:unmatched_returns,:error_handling,:no_opaque) will surface a pile of macro-generated findings. slackex's .dialyzer_ignore.exs is [] because slackex is clean. Add the tool + PLT + 'mix typecheck' alias, but keep dialyzer NON-blocking (continue-on-error or a separate non-required job) until .dialyzer_ignore.exs is triaged. That triage is the bulk of this ticket.
2. pgvector compile warnings: GroceryPlanner.PostgrexTypes (generated) emits compile warnings slackex never hits (no pgvector). .dialyzer_ignore.exs can't touch COMPILE warnings. Resolution tracked in the deploy-unblock pass (postgrex/ash_postgres bump).

## Deliverable
Credo --strict + Dialyzer (non-blocking) baked into mix aliases + unified ci-deploy.yml + lefthook pre-commit gate, matching the reference where it transfers. Do AFTER the deploy is green.
notes: CORRECTION to earlier note re Dialyzer: verified Ash gives the checker NO concrete type to work with, so Dialyzer would NOT catch the receipt.status class either. Evidence: (1) Inventory.create_receipt has 0 @specs at runtime (Code.Typespec.fetch_specs); (2) Ash models records as @type record :: struct() (generic), and per the docs resources 'do not define a type unless embedded — use :struct + instance_of'; (3) Ash's own history: #368 (2022) 'parameterizing all typespecs is a bigger effort' — never done; #2247 AshShape typed-projections proposal (2025) closed lukewarm. So a value from create_receipt is only ever 'struct()', and neither Elixir 1.19/1.20's checker nor Dialyzer can flag .status on 'some struct'. Typed code interfaces ({:ok, Receipt.t()} specs) are the only lever and are NOT on Ash's roadmap. => Dialyzer in l3d is still worth it for OTHER classes, but this specific 'stale field ref in an excluded test' class is only caught by running the integration tests or a rename-grep. Don't scope l3d as if Dialyzer closes it.

title: Port Skillet design system from Claude Design into app
description: Port the Skillet token layer (fairway/orchard/marble/dark themes, Instrument Serif + Inter type system, sk-tag/sk-card helpers) from the claude.ai/design project into app.css via daisyui-theme plugin blocks; self-host fonts; apply serif ramp to page_header/section/header components; replace 12-theme model with 4 themes incl. User validation, settings picker, and data migration of users.theme; sync design/ bundle back to repo and project.
close_reason: Skillet port shipped in bb01dcc: 4 themes via daisyui-theme blocks, self-hosted fonts, serif ramp on page_header/section/header, theme migration, design/ bundle mirrored

title: Review slackex DeepInfra usage for low-cost ML categorization
description: Owner uses DeepInfra in slackex for low-cost ML work. Review how slackex integrates it and decide whether grocery_planner's AI categorization (currently AI_CATEGORIZATION_ENABLED=false in prod — real local classification is out of CPU scope, distilbart ~1GB) should call DeepInfra instead of local models. Out of the homelab-migration map's scope (CPU-only sidecar is locked); post-migration backlog.

title: Flaky CI: ai_service_integration health_check times out
description: test/integration/ai_service_integration_test.exs:24 ('health/ready endpoint returns ok with dependency checks') intermittently fails in the 'Elixir ↔ Python Integration Tests' CI job with {:error, %Req.TransportError{reason: :timeout}} from AiClient.health_check(). Failed on e84a107 (a .beads-only commit), passed on a6dab94 minutes earlier — pure flake. Likely the Python sidecar isn't fully warm when the test runs, or the Req timeout is too tight for CI. Fix ideas: readiness-gate the sidecar in the workflow before mix test, and/or retry-with-backoff in the health check test path. Same run also logs Money.InvalidAmountError (float total_amount) in receipt processing — that belongs to the existing receipt-robustness P1, not this ticket.
close_reason: CI gate polled /health (bare liveness) which greenlights the instant uvicorn binds the port; the first integration test then races /health/ready (DB+model warm-up) → timeout. Fixed by gating on /health/ready and raising the wait cap 30→60s. Aligns with org-brain integration-tests-run-against-prod-identical-db (poll the real readiness signal, not liveness) and single-instance-ship-ready-defer-liveness (why the two endpoints differ). CPU-only torch (9b98724) had already shrunk the race window.

title: Design-system bundle + Claude Design sync for UI components
description: Scaffold a static HTML design twin of CoreComponents/UIComponents/Skeletons under design/, add design/ as a Tailwind @source, and sync to a fresh 'Grocery Planner' claude.ai/design project via DesignSync for UI/UX iteration.
close_reason: design/ bundle committed (a6dab94, CI green), synced to 'Grocery Planner' claude.ai/design project (18 files)

title: Task: align dev compose, CI, and CLAUDE.md on pgvector:pg18
description:
## Question

Surfaced by the Postgres image research (grocery_planner-tdu.1): prod will run pgvector/pgvector:pg18, so dev docker-compose.yml and the CI service image should move from pg16 to pg18 to keep all environments identical, and CLAUDE.md lines 53/131 should say PG18 is the *chosen* version, not a framework requirement (the claim was ash_postgres installer autodetect drift). Evidence: docs/research/prod-postgres-image.md on branch research/prod-postgres-image.

title: Task: remove Fly config from the repo
description:
## Question

Delete fly.toml, python_service/fly.toml, .github/workflows/deploy.yml and fly-deploy.yml (and any fly-only docs references). Only after the replacement deploy workflow is proven — the dormant Fly app itself is untouched.

title: Email digest rebuild-or-remove: wire daily digest as Oban cron worker, or drop the dead email toggle
description: Follow-up to grocery_planner-4a4, where the never-run Notifications.EmailScheduler GenServer (broken 3 ways, zero references, zero tests) was DELETED rather than fixed. What remains is a product gap: settings exposes email_notifications_enabled on NotificationPreference and users can enable it, but nothing sends email - a dead promise in the UI. Two options: (A) Rebuild the digest properly: Oban cron worker (Cron plugin already configured in config.exs with an empty job list), per-tenant reads via allow_global read action or per-account iteration with set_tenant, Ash.load!(pref, [:user, :account]) done correctly (single load, no N+1), send time derived from the account's timezone attribute instead of hardcoded 7AM UTC, from-address from config not hardcoded notifications@groceryplanner.com, and a test proving a digest is generated for a user with due notifications. The old HTML template and summary/recipe-row builders are recoverable from git history (lib/grocery_planner/notifications/email_scheduler.ex, deleted in the 4a4 fix). ExpirationAlerts and RecipeSuggestions modules still exist and are used by dashboard/meal planner. (B) Remove the email_notifications_enabled toggle from settings UI and the NotificationPreference resource until email digests are actually built. Decide based on whether email digest is on the product roadmap (see notifications backlog).
acceptance_criteria: Decision A or B recorded and implemented; no UI toggle exists that silently does nothing

title: Prep-day and freezer-meal support: batch cook, portion, drag back into the plan
description: Plan to Eat holds a near-monopoly on prepped-meal freezer inventory (Add to Freezer: count, servings, date; drag portions back onto future plan days) - absent from Mealime, Samsung Food, Mealie, Tandoor, Grocy, Paprika. Distinct from incidental leftovers (grocery_planner-def): this is DELIBERATE batch cooking - plan a prep day, cook 4x, freeze dated portions as inventory (freezer storage locations and freezable recipe flags already exist), then schedule those portions into future weeks. Completes the batch-cooking trio with servings scaling (cook mode bead) and cook-once-eat-many chains (already shipped).

title: Recipe nutrition via Open Food Facts: bounded macros, no medical claims
description: Auto-compute per-serving macros for recipes by joining ingredients to Open Food Facts nutrition data (keyed off existing categorization/barcodes), show per-recipe and per-planned-week totals, filter by high-protein etc. Research boundary: basic macro display is table stakes, but condition-specific medical planning (diabetic/FODMAP/renal) is explicitly deferred - LLM nutrition errors of 36-53 percent make it a liability without clinical framing. Feeds the natural-language planner's nutritional constraints.
notes: Priority raised P3->P2 and scope sharpened by final competitor deep-dive: the best-evidenced whitespace in the entire 10-capability analysis is the DAILY/WEEKLY NUTRITION ROLL-UP of the planned week vs a household goal (demand: Mealie #3743 top request, Grocy #413; supply: absent even in category leaders; PlateJoy did it and died for unrelated reasons). Per-recipe macros are the substrate; the plan-level roll-up vs goal is the differentiator. Also: import nutrition alongside the recipe when scraping (Tandoor #3391 - usually dropped by importers).

title: Mobile companion app on the existing offline-sync API
description: The purpose-built sync layer (delta pull, tombstones, atomic batch, optimistic concurrency, temp-id reconciliation) is a client contract waiting for a client. The recurring OSS ask is a mobile app / robust offline PWA - recipes and lists usable in-store and in-kitchen without signal. Decide the vehicle (installable PWA with local cache vs LiveView Native vs Flutter) and ship the two killer offline surfaces first: shopping list check-off and tonight's recipe. Sync reliability is what family-coordination apps live or die on - and the hard part is already built.

title: Home Assistant integration: sensors, notifications, Assist intents
description: Research verdict: arguably the highest-leverage single feature for the self-hosted family audience - Grocy's popularity is substantially BECAUSE of its HA add-on. Expose expiring-items count, low-stock list, tonight's planned meal, and shopping-list summary as HA sensors (REST/MQTT); push eat-me-first and expiry alerts through HA notifications to the devices the family already has; an Assist intent for what-can-I-make-tonight keeps voice local and private (vs Alexa in the kitchen). Plugs us into the hub this audience already runs.

title: Unit conversion + ingredient merge/dedup tooling
description: Recurring top-10 OSS request (Mealie 2024 survey): the same ingredient stored five ways breaks inventory matching, shopping aggregation, and can-I-make-this calculations. Admin merge tool for duplicate grocery items/units (re-pointing ingredients and entries), metric/imperial conversion on display and on shopping aggregation, normalization suggestions at import time (import creates chicken breast; you have Chicken Breasts - merge?). Data hygiene that quietly makes every other feature more accurate.

title: Cook mode: screen-awake guided steps with multi-timers and scaling
description: Paprika's most-loved in-kitchen feature: full-screen step-by-step instructions that keep the phone/tablet awake, inline multi-timers parsed from step text (simmer 20 min -> tap to start), ingredient quantities scaled to planned servings, step check-off. Makes the app present at the stove, not just at the desk - and is pure LiveView UI over data we already have.

title: Recipe cost per serving and cheap-tonight filter
description: Price each recipe from the household's own paid prices (fall back to Open Prices community data): cost per serving on recipe cards, week-plan total on the grocery-impact preview, and a sort-by-cheapest filter.
